title: Decimals on the number line: hundredths 0-0.1
description: Graph hundredths between 0 and 0.1 on a number line.
topic_path: Decimals on the number line: hundredths 0-0.1

assessment_items.question_content:
**Move the dot to $0.06$ on the number line.**

[[☃ number-line 1]]
hints:
The number line is divided into $10$ equal pieces between $0$ and $0.1$.

Each tick mark represents a length of $0.01$, or one hundredth.

Starting at $0$, we move a length of $\greenD{0.01}$ six times to get to $\goldE{0.06}$.



[[☃ image 1]]

The answer

[[☃ number-line 1]]

assessment_items.question_content:
**Move the dot to zero hundredths on the number line.**

[[☃ number-line 1]]
hints:
The number line is divided into $10$ equal pieces between $0$ and $0.1$.

Each tick mark represents a length of $0.01$, or one hundredth.

Since zero hundredths is the same as $0$, we keep the dot at $\goldE{0}$.



[[☃ image 1]]

The answer

[[☃ number-line 1]]

assessment_items.question_content:
**Move the dot to $0.10$ on the number line.**

[[☃ number-line 1]]
hints:
The number line is divided into $10$ equal pieces between $0$ and $0.1$.

Each tick mark represents a length of $0.01$, or one hundredth.

Starting at $0$, we move a length of $\greenD{0.01}$ ten times to get to $\goldE{0.10}$.


[[☃ image 1]]

Also, $\goldE{0.10}=\goldE{0.1}$ because $\goldE{\dfrac{10}{100}}=\goldE{\dfrac{1}{10}}$.

The answer

[[☃ number-line 1]]

assessment_items.question_content:
Look at the number line below. 

[[☃ image 2]]

**Where is the point on the number line?**  

[[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints:
The number line is divided into $10$ equal pieces between $0$ and $0.1$.

Each tick mark represents a length of $0.01$, or one hundredth.

Starting at $0$, we move a length of $\blueE{0.01}$ three times to get to the point.


[[☃ image 1]]
The point on the number line is $\maroonE{0.03}$.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following number lines show the point at 0.02?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
The length between the labeled tick marks is $\blueD{0.1}$, or $\blueD{1 \text{ tenth}}$.

Each $\blueD{0.1}$ is divided into $10$ equal pieces that have a length of $\goldD{0.01}$, or $\goldD{1 \text{ hundredth}}$.
$0.0\goldD2$ is $\goldD{ \text{2 hundredths}}$ to the right of $0$ on a number line.

Which number line graphs a point $\goldD{ \text{2 hundredths}}$ to the right of $0$?


[[☃ image 1]]

The number line that shows the point $0.02$ is



[[☃ image 1]]

![A number line labeled 0 to 0.1 with tick marks at every 0.01 unit. There is a blue dot labeled question mark at the second tick mark to the right of 0.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/9886909e2d483ced316a552fe358e5f53eeb206d)
